.ddx/attachments/ddx-ee2cd434/events.jsonl:12:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-ee2cd434 (SD-024 Stage 2)\n\n(a) WHAT IS DONE\n----------------\nThe previous attempt (commit 5ae02964) migrated the high-value `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`\nand `RunViaService(..., RunOptions{...})` test sites onto the SD-024 successor API\n(`r.RunWithConfig` + `RunWithConfigViaService` + `AgentRunRuntime`) across:\n\n  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go    (~22 sites: TestRunWith*, TestSessionLogging,\n                                         TestRunPromptFile, TestRunStdinMode,\n                                         TestRunModel*, TestRunNonZeroExit,\n                                         TestRunUnknownHarness, TestRunEmptyPrompt,\n                                         TestIntegration_CodexEcho, TestRunOpencode*,\n                                         and the *Permissions* pair)\n  - cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go   (lmstudio / openrouter dispatch regressions)\n  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via\n                                         TestRunConfigOpts and dispatches via\n                                         RunWithConfigViaService)\n\nThose 26 sites are the bulk of the \"test sites that exercise the high-level agent\nrun path\" called out in the bead description.\n\n(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING\n--------------------\nThe bead AC reads:\n\n    rg 'RunOptions\\{|CompareOptions\\{|QuorumOptions\\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside\n    the type definitions about to be retired.\n\nThis is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires\nretiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test\ncode. The remaining sites fall into two classes:\n\nCLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the\nRunOptions construction is not a \"test site\" the test could rewrite without\nchanging the function under test:\n\n  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites\n                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.\n  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go\n        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt\n        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper\n        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper\n  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go\n        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)\n        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})\n  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go\n        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})\n  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go\n        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})\n  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go\n        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures\n\nMigrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the\nproduction function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,\nRunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,\nplus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,\nexplicitly outside the scope the bead description names.\n\nCLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:\n\n  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462\n  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786\n  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201\n  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976\n  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)\n  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685\n  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531\n\nUntil these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they\nflow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.\n\nThe fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:\n\n    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for\n    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,\n    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,\n    (c) production-code migration,\n    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.\n\nThat assessment is correct. The bead title (\"5-10 sites\") and description (\"migrate\ntest sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions\") describe a\ntest-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not\ncompatible inside one bead.\n\n(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED\n---------------------------------------\nEither:\n\n  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.\n\n      \"rg 'RunOptions\\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns\n      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService.\"\n\n      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.\n\n  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. .ddx/attachments/ddx-fdd3ea36/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes\nrationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)\n\n## Decision\n\nStopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a\nmulti-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it\nin one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that\nfails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config\nschema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs\nwithout the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.\n\n## What is already done (verified in this worktree)\n\n1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under\n   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in\n   `closed` status:\n     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)\n     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)\n     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)\n     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)\n     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)\n     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)\n\n2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**\n   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns\n   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)\n   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1\n   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.\n\n## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass\n\n### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative\n\nAC #1 asks for \"a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class\ndefault and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +\ngating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost\ndimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback.\"\n\nReading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:\n  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.\n  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.\n  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,\n    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent\n    \"routing point release\" narrative.\n\nThe closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as\nside-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the\ncombined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires\neither (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a \"v0.9.25 — routing\npoint release\" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead\ndescription must be relaxed to accept \"the code is in v0.9.23, even if\nno single CHANGELOG block summarizes it.\" Either resolution is a\ngovernance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.\n\n### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)\n\nThese criteria collectively require:\n  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one\n    `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call with no Model / no Harness /\n    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;\n  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim\n    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);\n  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,\n    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,\n    with one-time process warnings;\n  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to\n    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;\n  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /\n    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with\n    dead-code detection in CI;\n  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn\n    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override\n    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness\n    conflict);\n  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's\n    real config;\n  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.\n\nThis is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:\n  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`\n  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)\n  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)\n  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)\n  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)\n\nThat work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead\nexplicitly bundles into \"one DDx PR\" — but a PR of that size needs\narchitectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking\nconfig change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the\nfirst line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC\ndeterministically, not to make architecture decisions.\n\n### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads\n\nThe bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:\n  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,\n  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)\n    stays independent.\n\nFiling beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the\nproject's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is\nmechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.\n\n### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden\n\nA net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical\n19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test\n(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not\nhave gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nTo make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead\nmust be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:\n\n1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever\n   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add\n   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially\n   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.\n\n2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default\n   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:\n   \"default\")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.\n\n3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before\n   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded\n   incompatibility.\n\n4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on\n   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +\n   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n\n5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override\n   combination.\n\n6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test\n   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.\n\n7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of\n   compensating logic.\n\n8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.\n\n9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI\n   bead.\n\nEach of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The\ncurrent bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one\nattempt.\n\n## Recommendation to the orchestrator\n\nReopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into\nthe nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three\n(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated\ngoals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") suggests\nthis bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as\nthe deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.\nresult_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nbase_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nretry_after=2026-04-29T04:30:22Z","created_at":"2026-04-28T22:30:22.575970819Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"no_changes"}

# Bead Authoring Template

> A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent
> sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation.
> — Reliability principle **P7** (`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`)

This document is the canonical template + rubric for creating beads in
this repository. It exists because the 2026-05-03 bead-quality audit
(`.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`)
found that only **1 of 20** representative open beads scored 8/8 on
the sufficient-sub-agent-prompt rubric. The most common failure modes
were: no specific test names in AC (80 % miss), no `go test` +
lefthook gate in AC (80 % miss), and no root cause with file:line in
description (65 % miss).

---

## 1. The 8-criterion rubric

Every bead must satisfy:

| # | Criterion | Verifier |
|---|-----------|----------|
| (a) | **Title**: one-line scope clarity, imperative, names subsystem + change | "Pagination off-by-one in bead list endpoint" not "Pagination bug" |
| (b) | **Description**: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope | Root cause cites `cli/path/file.go:42-50` |
| (c) | **AC**: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names | At least one AC names `TestFoo` or a `go test -run` filter |
| (d) | **AC** includes "wired-in" assertion when introducing new code paths | New function → AC asserts a caller invokes it (production or test) |
| (e) | **AC** includes `go test` command + lefthook gate | Both `cd cli && go test ./...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` named |
| (f) | **Labels**: phase + area + kind + cross-refs | `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus adr/spec/prevention if applicable |
| (g) | **Parent + Deps**: explicit | Parent set; deps either listed or "no deps" stated |
| (h) | Description reads as **sufficient sub-agent prompt** | A competent agent given only the bead body can pick a file and run tests without asking |

Score 8/8 = execution-ready. Less = retrofit before dispatch.

---

## 2. Template fields

```text
TITLE
  <area>: <imperative one-line scope>
  e.g. "agent: unify ExecuteLoopSpec across cobra/HTTP/server layers"

TYPE
  task | bug | epic | chore

LABELS
  phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>
  + cross-refs: prevention, adr:<id>, spec:<id>, story:<n>, refactor

PARENT
  <ddx-id> of governing epic / story / spec
  (omit only if this is a root)

DEPS
  - <ddx-id-1>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  - <ddx-id-2>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  (or explicit "No deps.")

SPEC-ID (when applicable)
  --set spec-id=FEAT-NNN  | SD-NNN | TD-NNN | ADR-NNN

DESCRIPTION
  PROBLEM
    <one-paragraph statement of what is broken or missing>

  ROOT CAUSE
    <function/file:line where the defect lives>
    <what the code does today vs what it should do>
    For new features: ROOT CAUSE -> "no existing implementation"
    is OK; replace with CURRENT STATE describing the gap.

  PROPOSED FIX
    <bulleted concrete changes; cite file:line for each>
    - cli/internal/foo/bar.go:120-145  rename Baz -> Quux
    - cli/internal/foo/bar_test.go      add TestQuux_RoundTrip

  NON-SCOPE
    - <thing readers might assume is included but isn't>
    - <thing covered by sister bead ddx-NNNN>

  INTERSECTIONS (optional)
    - <ddx-id>: <how this bead relates to that one>

ACCEPTANCE
  1. <verifiable assertion 1 — names a Test, file path, command, or
     observable artifact>
  2. <verifiable assertion 2>
  3. ...
  N-1. cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green.
  N. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 3. Examples

### 3.1 Example of a strong bead (audit score 8/8)

`ddx-1e516bc9` — *"fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models"*

Why it scored 8/8:

- **Title (a)** names subsystem (`fizeau Execute`), the precondition (`explicit Harness + empty Model`), and the expected behavior (`route within harness's eligible models`).
- **Description (b)** opens with **OBSERVED** (one paragraph, file:line) → **SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY** → **INVESTIGATION FIRST** (with explicit close-as-no-op outcome) → **UPSTREAM CHANGE** (file:line into `service_execute.go:193-224`, `models.yaml:31-60`, `registry.go`) → explicit **NOT IN SCOPE** list naming five excluded paths.
- **AC (c)** names two concrete tests:
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness` and
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly`.
- **Wired-in (d)**: AC #6 asserts the resolved model surfaces in
  `.ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json`, `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json`, and the
  workers UI — three distinct call-graph endpoints.
- **Test command + lefthook (e)**: AC #9 — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.`
- **Labels (f)**: `phase:2, area:agent, kind:fix, upstream-fizeau, observed-failure`.
- **Parent + Deps (g)**: parent `ddx-e34994e2`; dep `ddx-cfedee8e` with explanation that the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on.
- **Sufficient (h)**: a sub-agent given only the bead body knows which file in fizeau to read, what the gap looks like, what the negative-test outcome should be, and how to verify the end-to-end fix without asking.

### 3.2 Example of a weak bead (audit score 1/8) — annotated

`ddx-0793ac75` — *"federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields"*

Verbatim description:
```
GraphQL resolvers that aggregate across spokes (federatedWorkers,
federatedRuns, federatedDocuments, etc.). Add routing-metadata fields
exposing which spoke owns each result. Write-routing contract: hub
forwards mutations to owning node, never broadcasts (Story 15
dependency).
```

Verbatim AC:
```
1. federated* resolvers in schema.
2. Routing-metadata visible per result.
3. Mutations route to owner; tested.
4. Tests cover happy + degraded paths.
```

Failures, criterion by criterion:

- **(b) FAIL** — no problem statement (what's broken today?), no root cause, no file:line, no non-scope. The reader cannot tell where the schema lives, where resolvers go, or what "routing-metadata" should look like as a Go type.
- **(c) FAIL** — "tested" and "Tests cover happy + degraded paths" name no `Test*` function. Sub-agent has no way to know what to write or where.
- **(d) PARTIAL** — "tested" implies the resolvers are wired, but no production-call-graph assertion (e.g., "schema.graphql Mutation block exposes field X").
- **(e) FAIL** — no `go test` command, no lefthook gate.
- **(h) FAIL** — a sub-agent reading this cold cannot answer: which schema file? which resolver package? what fields on routing-metadata? what's the wire format? Hand-curation by the operator is the only path forward.

**Retrofit shape** (illustrative — not an actual edit):
```
PROBLEM
  Story 14 needs aggregate-across-spokes resolvers. Today the GraphQL
  schema (cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql) exposes only
  per-node Worker/Run/Document queries. Federation requires
  federated* counterparts that fan out to spokes and stitch results.

ROOT CAUSE
  No federated resolver layer exists. cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go
  contains only single-node resolvers. Spoke fan-out is in
  cli/internal/server/federation_spoke.go but nothing in the GraphQL
  layer calls it.

PROPOSED FIX
  - schema.graphql: add Federated{Worker,Run,Document} types with
    `ownerSpoke: String!` field.
  - graphql_resolver.go: add federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
    federatedDocuments resolvers calling federation_spoke.FanOut.
  - graphql_mutation.go: write-routing helper routes mutations to
    owning node via federation_spoke.RouteToOwner.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (out: covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery (out: covered by ddx-fe63969f).

ACCEPTANCE
  1. schema.graphql exposes federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
     federatedDocuments queries with ownerSpoke metadata.
  2. TestFederatedWorkers_FanOutAggregates verifies fan-out + stitch.
  3. TestFederatedMutation_RoutesToOwner verifies write-routing.
  4. TestFederatedWorkers_DegradedSpoke returns partial results +
     warning.
  5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
  6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 4. Authoring checklist (paste into your editor before running `ddx bead create`)

```text
[ ] (a) Title: imperative, one line, names subsystem + change
[ ] (b) Description has all four sub-elements:
        [ ] PROBLEM (one paragraph)
        [ ] ROOT CAUSE with file:line citation (or CURRENT STATE for new features)
        [ ] PROPOSED FIX (bulleted, file:line per item)
        [ ] NON-SCOPE
[ ] (c) Each AC line is verifiable; at least one names Test* or `go test -run`
[ ] (d) New code path → AC asserts a production or test caller invokes it
[ ] (e) AC includes:
        [ ] cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green
        [ ] lefthook run pre-commit passes
[ ] (f) Labels: phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>, +cross-refs
[ ] (g) Parent set; deps listed or "no deps" stated
[ ] (h) Self-test: re-read the bead from cold. Could a competent
        sub-agent execute it without asking a question? If no, retrofit.
[ ] No /tmp/ paths cited as load-bearing context. Inline the relevant
    excerpt instead — /tmp does not survive between machines.
```

---

## 5. Anti-patterns surfaced by the audit

- **`/tmp/` references as load-bearing context.** 5 of 20 audited beads cite a `/tmp/...` plan file. These are functionally broken links to a sub-agent on a different machine. **Inline the excerpt.**
- **"See parent" without inline summary.** Refactor children of `ddx-5cb6e6cd` (C4/C7/C11/C13) systematically defer detail to the parent epic. Each child fails the stand-alone test. **Inline the parent's relevant 5-10 lines.**
- **Vague test phrases.** "Tests cover X." "All execute-bead tests still pass." Both omit the actual `Test*` name or `-run` filter the executor needs.
- **Story-doc copy-paste.** One-paragraph beads (`ddx-0793ac75`, `ddx-0e5c3005`, `ddx-1d52a30c`, `ddx-0ca4ffda`) are story excerpts that were never expanded for execution. Story documents are *upstream* of beads, not the bead itself.
- **Implicit lefthook.** Lefthook runs in pre-commit hooks anyway, but if AC doesn't name it, executors omit it from their own verification before committing.

---

## 6. When to skip a criterion
